perturbation.original_text: Either Party may disclose Confidential Information to the other Party in confidence provided that the disclosing Party identifies such information as proprietary and confidential either by marking it, in the case of written materials, or, in the case of information that is disclosed orally or written materials that are not marked, by notifying the other Party of the proprietary and confidential nature of the information, such notification to be done orally, by email or written correspondence, or via other means of communication as might be appropriate.
perturbation.changed_text: Either Party may disclose Confidential Information to the other Party in confidence provided that the disclosing Party identifies such information as proprietary and confidential by marking it, in the case of written materials, and by notifying the other Party of the proprietary and confidential nature of the information, such notification to be done orally, by email or written correspondence, or via other means of communication as might be appropriate.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'or, in the case of information that is disclosed orally or written materials that are not marked', the modification implies that oral and unmarked written materials are no longer considered Confidential Information unless they are explicitly marked, conflicting with the initial definition that includes oral disclosures. This change introduces ambiguity about what constitutes Confidential Information if it's not marked, creating uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: When informed of the proprietary and confidential nature of Confidential Information that has been disclosed by the other Party, the receiving Party ("Recipient") shall, for a period of three (3) years from the date of disclosure, refrain from disclosing such Confidential Information to any contractor or other third party without prior, written approval from the disclosing Party and shall protect such Confidential Information from inadvertent disclosure to a third party using the same care and diligence that the Recipient uses to protect its own proprietary and confidential information, but in no case less than reasonable care. The Recipient shall ensure that each of its employees, officers, directors, or agents who has access to Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement is informed of its proprietary and confidential nature and is required to abide by the terms of this Agreement. The Recipient of Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall promptly notify the disclosing Party of any disclosure of such Confidential Information in violation of this Agreement or of any subpoena or other legal process requiring production or disclosure of said Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the 'or, in the case of information that is disclosed orally or written materials that are not marked' clause. This creates a contradiction because it now implies that only marked written materials are considered confidential, while the contradicted text still refers to the protection of Confidential Information disclosed orally without necessarily being marked. The change introduces ambiguity regarding unmarked oral disclosures, directly conflicting with the requirement in the contradicted text to protect such information.

perturbation.original_text: When informed of the proprietary and confidential nature of Confidential Information that has been disclosed by the other Party, the receiving Party ("Recipient") shall, for a period of three (3) years from the date of disclosure, refrain from disclosing such Confidential Information to any contractor or other third party without prior, written approval from the disclosing Party and shall protect such Confidential Information from inadvertent disclosure to a third party using the same care and diligence that the Recipient uses to protect its own proprietary and confidential information, but in no case less than reasonable care.
perturbation.changed_text: When informed of the proprietary and confidential nature of Confidential Information that has been disclosed by the other Party, the receiving Party ("Recipient") shall, for a period of one (1) year from the date of disclosure, refrain from disclosing such Confidential Information to any contractor or other third party without prior, written approval from the disclosing Party and shall protect such Confidential Information from inadvertent disclosure to a third party using the same care and diligence that the Recipient uses to protect its own proprietary and confidential information, but in no case less than reasonable care.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the duration from three years to one year creates an in-text contradiction because elsewhere in the agreement (specifically in Section 9) it states the agreement is in effect for two years. This directly contradicts the duration for which the recipient must keep information confidential, creating uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall remain in effect for a period of two (2) years from the Effective Date unless otherwise terminated by either Party giving notice to the other of its desire to terminate this Agreement. The requirement to protect Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall survive termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the confidentiality period from three years to one year. The contradicted text states that the agreement is in effect for two years and that the requirement to protect Confidential Information survives termination. Therefore, the change creates an in-text contradiction because the one-year confidentiality period in the changed text is shorter than both the agreement's term (two years) and the survival period of the confidentiality obligation, creating uncertainty and potential conflict.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient shall honor any request from the disclosing Party to promptly return or destroy all copies of Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement and all notes related to such Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient may honor any request from the disclosing Party to promptly return or destroy copies of Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement and all notes related to such Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "shall" to "may," the obligation to return or destroy confidential information becomes discretionary rather than mandatory. This contradicts the intended protection of Confidential Information, as the Recipient now has the option to retain it even after a request, undermining the confidentiality agreement and creates uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the disclosing Party and nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as granting or conferring any rights to such Confidential Information on the other Party. The Recipient may honor any request from the disclosing Party to promptly return or destroy copies of Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement and all notes related to such Confidential Information. The Parties agree that the disclosing Party will suffer irreparable injury if its Confidential Information is made public, released to a third party, or otherwise disclosed in breach of this Agreement and that the disclosing Party shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against a threatened breach or continuation of any such breach and, in the event of such breach, an award of actual and exemplary damages from any court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.justification: The change from "shall" to "may" alters the Recipient's obligation regarding the return or destruction of confidential information from mandatory to discretionary. The Contradicted Text specifies that "All Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the disclosing Party" and that the disclosing party will suffer "irreparable injury" if the confidential information is disclosed. Allowing the recipient to optionally return or destroy copies directly contradicts the disclosing party's ownership and the measures to prevent disclosure, creating an inconsistency and potentially undermining the agreement's confidentiality provisions as the recipient may now choose to retain confidential information, increasing the risk of unauthorized disclosure. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: The terms of this Agreement shall not be construed to limit either Party's right to develop independently or acquire products without use of the other Party's Confidential Information. The disclosing party acknowledges that the Recipient may currently or in the future be developing information internally, or receiving information from other parties, that is similar to the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: The terms of this Agreement shall be construed to limit either Party's right to develop independently or acquire products with use of the other Party's Confidential Information. The disclosing party acknowledges that the Recipient may currently or in the future be developing information internally, or receiving information from other parties, that is similar to the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "shall not" to "shall" and “without use” to “with use” in Section 4, the agreement now states that the agreement limits a party's right to develop products using the other party's Confidential Information. This directly contradicts the fundamental purpose of a non-disclosure agreement, which is to allow development while protecting confidential information. This internal contradiction can make the agreement unenforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The terms of this Agreement shall be construed to limit either Party's right to develop independently or acquire products with use of the other Party's Confidential Information. The disclosing party acknowledges that the Recipient may currently or in the future be developing information internally, or receiving information from other parties, that is similar to the Confidential Information. Nothing in this Agreement will prohibit the Recipient from developing or having developed for it products, concepts, systems or techniques that are similar to or compete with the products, concepts, systems or techniques contemplated by or embodied in the Confidential Information provided that the Recipient does not violate any of its obligations under this Agreement in connection with such development.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the agreement limits a party's right to develop products with the use of the other party's Confidential Information. The contradicted text states 'Nothing in this Agreement will prohibit the Recipient from developing or having developed for it products...provided that the Recipient does not violate any of its obligations under this Agreement in connection with such development.' This means that according to the changed text, the party cannot develop or acquire products with the other party's confidential information, but the contradicted text states that the party can develop products, concepts, systems or techniques that are similar to or compete with the products, concepts, systems or techniques contemplated by or embodied in the Confidential Information. The change therefore, introduces a direct and impactful contradiction within the document, adhering to the Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall remain in effect for a period of two (2) years from the Effective Date unless otherwise terminated by either Party giving notice to the other of its desire to terminate this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall remain in effect for a period of two (2) months from the Effective Date unless otherwise terminated by either Party giving notice to the other of its desire to terminate this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By reducing the effective period of the agreement from 'two (2) years' to 'two (2) months' but not changing the confidentiality period of three years stated in section 2, an inconsistency is created. It directly contradicts the defined period in which both parties are obligated to maintain confidentiality, leading to confusion about the duration of the agreement’s restrictions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: When informed of the proprietary and confidential nature of Confidential Information that has been disclosed by the other Party, the receiving Party ("Recipient") shall, for a period of three (3) years from the date of disclosure, refrain from disclosing such Confidential Information to any contractor or other third party without prior, written approval from the disclosing Party and shall protect such Confidential Information from inadvertent disclosure to a third party using the same care and diligence that the Recipient uses to protect its own proprietary and confidential information, but in no case less than reasonable care. The Recipient shall ensure that each of its employees, officers, directors, or agents who has access to Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement is informed of its proprietary and confidential nature and is required to abide by the terms of this Agreement. The Recipient of Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall promptly notify the disclosing Party of any disclosure of such Confidential Information in violation of this Agreement or of any subpoena or other legal process requiring production or disclosure of said Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text reduces the agreement duration from two years to two months. However, the contradicted text states a confidentiality period of three years from the date of disclosure. This creates a direct conflict, as the confidentiality obligations extend significantly beyond the new, shorter agreement duration, making it an in-text contradiction.
